summary: A strong ’87 Hampton show with a smoking Sugar Magnolia→Scarlet→Fire opener, captured on a matrix tape that leans heavy on audience ambience.
review: The concert is widely praised for its high‑energy first night of the spring‑’87 run – Jerry’s return was felt in every note, the pre‑drums “Sugar Magnolia > Scarlet > Fire” sequence is called legendary, and songs like Cassidy, The Wheel and Black Peter get special shout‑outs.  The recording is a matrix source that favors audience ambience; listeners note it isn’t a pristine soundboard but still conveys the excitement of the night.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers highlight an outstanding performance (≈4‑star show quality) while noting a mixed‑quality matrix recording (≈3‑star). Weighted 70/30 the combined score lands around 3.8.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Heavy audience ambience due to Healy’s matrix mix; some say it’s just above a high‑quality audience recording, others call the ambience excessive. No major dropouts reported, but the sound isn’t as clear as a pure SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Opening night of the spring ’87 run, tight and upbeat.  The pre‑drums “Sugar Magnolia > Scarlet > Fire” sequence set a high bar, maintaining momentum through strong mid‑set numbers and an uplifting second‑set finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for being ‘alive’, energetic guitar work and infectious vibe; several reviewers note his tone and excitement.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for excellent rhythm/lead guitar on The Wheel and overall solid playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards are mentioned positively as part of the renewed energy of the band in ’87.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as fine; the pre‑drums sequence is noted for its excitement.
